should_trigger: Design the runtime architecture for an agent orchestrator with many workers, queues, model providers, tool sandboxes, and a replay store.
Our orchestrator sometimes runs the same tool call twice after retries. Review the distributed-system design for idempotency, deduplication, and message delivery semantics.
We need to split planning, scheduling, execution, and recovery across services. Decide state ownership, consistency rules, and coordination boundaries.
A worker can crash after calling an external API but before committing step status. Design recovery so the run does not overspend budget or repeat unsafe side effects.
Review this multi-tenant orchestrator topology for hot partitions, stale leases, noisy tenants, and partial failure behavior.
should_not_trigger: Summarize Tanenbaum and van Steen's Distributed Systems book for a reading group.
Pick the cheapest Kubernetes hosting provider for a small web app.
Write a single-process Python task runner with no queues, workers, replicas, or remote dependencies.
edge_cases: We have one local worker but want to add retry logic. Use this skill only if the retries cross durable state, queues, external side effects, or future multi-worker behavior.
We need SLO dashboards for a distributed orchestrator. Trigger this skill for distributed failure assumptions and state boundaries, then use an observability skill for dashboard and alert design.

compliance_expectations.should_trigger: Identify distributed boundaries, durable IDs, state ownership, coordination needs, messaging semantics, partial-failure handling, consistency decisions, scaling/placement, and verification drills.
compliance_expectations.should_not_trigger: Do not apply this skill to book summaries, vendor shopping, or local-only task runners with no distributed correctness concern.
compliance_expectations.edge_cases: Trigger only when retry or observability questions depend on durable state, queues, external effects, or distributed failure assumptions; otherwise defer to a narrower skill.
compliance_expectations.runtime_dependency: The response must use SKILL.md as the complete runtime knowledge source and must not ask to read external files, webpages, original books, source reports, external source folders, or source-pack material.
